content.text:
Memory stage: ClaimReconciler v1.
        Runtime processing time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate claims should update active memory and assign predicate policy.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_claim_id": "existing-claim-id-or-null",
              "canonical_predicate": "stable_snake_case_predicate_or_null",
              "predicate_family": "stable_conceptual_family_or_null",
              "predicate_description": "short policy meaning or null",
              "object_kind": "entity | string | number | boolean | json | null",
              "cardinality": "single | multi | null",
              "temporal_policy": "atemporal | time_scoped | status_like | null",
              "conflict_policy": "replace | coexist | range_split | null",
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new durable information.
        - noop: candidate is duplicate, low-value, or should not become active memory.
        - supersede: candidate is the new current value and target_claim_id is the old active claim it replaces.
        - retract: target_claim_id should stop being active because the target message retracts it without a replacement candidate.
        - update: target_claim_id should be patched without adding a new candidate; use sparingly.

        Predicate policy fields:
        - canonical_predicate: normalized predicate to store for the candidate. Reuse an existing predicate only if it is the same semantic relation; otherwise choose a concise snake_case relation.
        - predicate_family: stable conceptual family used for future reconciliation.
        - cardinality: single if one subject can have only one active value in this family and scope; multi if several values can coexist.
        - temporal_policy: atemporal for timeless facts, time_scoped for facts true in explicit time windows, status_like for current states, current preferences, decisions, and other evolving values.
        - conflict_policy: replace when a new value invalidates the old active value, coexist when both can remain true, range_split when old and new values are both temporally meaningful.

        Rules:
        - Use the target source and full thread context to understand corrections, replacements, pronouns, temporal meaning, and scope.
        - Do not decide by literal predicate names. Infer the semantic predicate family from the claim meaning, subject, object, qualifiers, evidence, scope, and temporal fields.
        - Use an existing predicate from Predicate catalog excerpt when it captures the same semantic relation.
        - If a catalog predicate fits, set canonical_predicate to that exact predicate and copy its cardinality, temporal_policy, and conflict_policy.
        - Invent a new canonical_predicate only when no active catalog predicate captures the relation.
        - Never canonicalize to generic description, classification, property-bag, or paraphrase predicates such as "is_described_as", "has_property", "is_a", or "defined_as". Choose a specific durable relation or return noop.
        - For ordinary positive preferences, use catalog predicate "prefers"; do not keep category-specific variants such as "prefers_car_brand" unless they represent a named current/default slot.
        - Do not map a named current/default/primary/chosen/backend slot to a generic preference, affinity, status, or usage predicate; use or create the specific slot predicate.
        - For named metric, record, score, benchmark, quota, threshold, or personal-best values, use catalog predicate "current_metric_value" when present. Preserve the metric/domain in scope or qualifiers so unrelated metrics do not replace each other.
        - For current place, residence, base, or relocation-result values, use catalog predicate "current_location" when present. It is a status-like single-value slot for the same subject and scope; newer explicit current-location values replace older active values.
        - For concrete details inside imported generated artifacts, use catalog predicate "generated_artifact_detail" when no more specific catalog predicate fits. These details usually coexist unless a later artifact version explicitly supersedes an earlier version.
        - If the target source updates or replaces an existing value, keep the semantic slot/family from the replaced claim when the source and context identify that slot.
        - If multiple candidates from the same target source express the same durable point at different abstraction levels, keep the most specific/actionable candidate and return noop for weaker broader candidates.
        - If one candidate captures the target source's explicit current/default/status value and another candidate is only a generic preference or affinity inferred from the same words, return noop for the generic candidate unless the target source independently asserts it.
        - Do not insert redundant broader candidates merely because they use a different predicate or family.
        - For exclusive current/default/status values in the same subject, scope, and environment, use cardinality=single and conflict_policy=replace unless simultaneous active values are explicitly allowed.
        - For dated observations or historical phase facts, use temporal_policy=time_scoped; they can coexist with later current-state claims when scopes differ by time, phase, or environment.
        - Runtime processing time is not evidence that a pending obligation has expired, completed, or become stale. For catalog pending_* claims and other independently completeable duties, insert the explicit pending fact unless the target source or existing active memory explicitly says it was completed, cancelled, returned, picked up, superseded, or otherwise no longer pending.
        - If the candidate is equivalent to an existing active claim in the same semantic family and scope, return noop.
        - If predicate policy says replace and the candidate conflicts with an older active value in the same family and scope, supersede the old claim.
        - If predicate policy says coexist, insert the candidate unless it is a duplicate.
        - If predicate policy says range_split, insert the candidate and supersede only an old current-state claim that should no longer be retrieved as current.
        - Attributed viewpoints, independent owners, independent events, and historical facts usually coexist unless the target source explicitly retracts them.
        - Use catalog predicate "believes" for third-party viewpoints such as "Alice thinks X"; do not canonicalize those claims to "prefers" or "avoids" unless the source explicitly asserts preference or avoidance.
        - Prefer insert over destructive actions when policy or evidence is unclear.
        - Return one operation for each candidate unless the right action is a target-only retract/update.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate claims below.
        - target_claim_id must be one of Existing active claims below or null.
        - Fill predicate policy fields for every operation with a candidate, including noop.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 11. Write path .. 12. Read path
Document lines: 329-466

## 11. Write path

### Step 1. Persist source
Сохраняем raw input:
- text
- source type
- speaker
- timestamp
- namespace
- thread/conversation ids
- optional embedding

### Step 2. Route
Router решает:
- `noop`
- `direct_structured_write`
- `note_write`
- `mixed`

### Step 3. Retrieve candidate memories
Перед extraction достаем:
- похожие claims
- похожие notes
- open tasks
- existing profile
- candidate entities / aliases
- recent evidence

### Step 4. Canonicalize entities
Привязать mentions к existing entities или создать новые.

### Step 5. Extract
Разные prompts для:
- claim extraction
- note construction
- task update
- profile update

### Step 6. Reconcile
Сравниваем candidate items с existing items.

### Step 7. Apply transaction
Одной транзакцией:
- upsert entities
- apply claim ops
- apply note ops
- apply task ops
- update profile projection
- store memory run log

### Step 8. Schedule maintenance
По debounce или cron:
- note consolidation
- duplicate merge
- stale cleanup
- profile repair
- alias merge
- retention / archive

## 12. Read path

### Step 1. Decide whether memory is needed
Memory search нужен, если вопрос:
- user-specific
- history-aware
- task-aware
- rationale-heavy
- temporal
- high-stakes

### Step 2. Load core blocks
Подтягиваем:
- short profile
- active tasks
- optional session summary

### Step 3. Choose retrieval mode

#### Claim-first
Для:
- factual questions
- preferences
- current status
- short “кто/что/когда”

#### Note-first
Для:
- “почему решили?”
- “к чему склонялись?”
- “что обсуждали?”
- “какой паттерн уже был?”

#### Mixed
Для:
- factual + rationale
- history + current state
- task with supporting discussion

#### Evidence-first / evidence-fallback
Для:
- конфликтов
- запросов на подтверждение / цитату
- высокой ставки

### Step 4. Respect retrieval budget
`v3` требует явных budget caps.

Рекомендуемый старт:

- `factual`
  - up to `6 claims`
  - up to `2 tasks`
  - up to `2 evidence snippets`

- `rationale`
  - up to `4 notes`
  - up to `2 claims`
  - up to `4 evidence snippets`

- `task`
  - up to `4 tasks`
  - up to `2 notes`
  - up to `2 evidence snippets`

- `mixed`
  - up to `4 claims`
  - up to `3 notes`
  - up to `2 tasks`
  - up to `3 evidence snippets`

### Step 5. Compose answer
Answer composer обязан:
- различать verified vs provisional
- учитывать conflicts
- уважать scope boundaries
- уметь abstain
- не выдавать note как hard fact без оговорки

        Candidate claims:
        - candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<0> predicate=stores_raw_evidence object="raw input text, source type, speaker, timestamp, namespace, thread/conversation ids, and optional embedding" scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 11 Write path validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The Write path persists raw input evidence, including text, source type, speaker, timestamp, namespace, thread or conversation IDs, and optional embedding. evidence=Сохраняем raw input: - text - source type - speaker - timestamp - namespace - thread/conversation ids - optional embedding
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<0> predicate=has_constraint object="The Write path retrieves candidate memories before extraction, including similar claims, similar notes, open tasks, existing profile, candidate entities or aliases, and recent evidence." scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 11 Write path validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The Write path must retrieve candidate memories before extraction. evidence=Перед extraction достаем: - похожие claims - похожие notes - open tasks - existing profile - candidate entities / aliases - recent evidence
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<0> predicate=has_constraint object="The Write path canonicalizes entities by linking mentions to existing entities or creating new ones." scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 11 Write path validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The Write path canonicalizes entities by linking mentions to existing entities or creating new entities. evidence=Привязать mentions к existing entities или создать новые.
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<0> predicate=has_constraint object="The Write path applies entity upserts, claim operations, note operations, task operations, profile projection updates, and memory run log storage in one transaction." scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 11 Write path validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The Write path applies memory updates in one transaction. evidence=Одной транзакцией: - upsert entities - apply claim ops - apply note ops - apply task ops - update profile projection - store memory run log
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<1> predicate=has_constraint object="Memory search is needed for user-specific, history-aware, task-aware, rationale-heavy, temporal, or high-stakes questions." scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 12 Read path validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The Read path uses memory search when a question is user-specific, history-aware, task-aware, rationale-heavy, temporal, or high-stakes. evidence=Memory search нужен, если вопрос: - user-specific - history-aware - task-aware - rationale-heavy - temporal - high-stakes
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<2> predicate=answers_question object="factual questions, preferences, current status, and short who/what/when questions" scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 12 Read path validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Claim-first retrieval mode is used for factual questions, preferences, current status, and short who/what/when questions. evidence=Claim-first Для: - factual questions - preferences - current status - short “кто/что/когда”
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<3> predicate=answers_question object="why a decision was made, what the system leaned toward, what was discussed, and what pattern already existed" scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 12 Read path validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Note-first retrieval mode is used for rationale, prior leanings, discussion history, and previously observed patterns. evidence=Note-first Для: - “почему решили?” - “к чему склонялись?” - “что обсуждали?” - “какой паттерн уже был?”
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<4> predicate=answers_question object="questions combining facts with rationale, history with current state, or tasks with supporting discussion" scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 12 Read path validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Mixed retrieval mode is used for questions combining factual and rationale needs, history and current state, or tasks with supporting discussion. evidence=Mixed Для: - factual + rationale - history + current state - task with supporting discussion
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<5> predicate=answers_question object="conflicts, requests for confirmation or quotation, and high-stakes questions" scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 12 Read path validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Evidence-first or evidence-fallback retrieval mode is used for conflicts, confirmation or quote requests, and high-stakes questions. evidence=Evidence-first / evidence-fallback Для: - конфликтов - запросов на подтверждение / цитату - высокой ставки
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<1> predicate=has_constraint object="Memory v3 requires explicit retrieval budget caps in the Read path." scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 12 Read path validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The Read path in Memory v3 requires explicit retrieval budget caps. evidence=`v3` требует явных budget caps.
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<1> predicate=has_constraint object="The Read path answer composer must distinguish verified versus provisional information, account for conflicts, respect scope boundaries, be able to abstain, and avoid presenting a note as a hard fact without qualification." scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 12 Read path validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The Read path answer composer must distinguish verified from provisional information, account for conflicts, respect scope boundaries, abstain when needed, and avoid treating notes as hard facts without qualification. evidence=Answer composer обязан: - различать verified vs provisional - учитывать conflicts - уважать scope boundaries - уметь abstain - не выдавать note как hard fact без оговорки

        Existing active claims:
        - id=hot-path:claim:<0> subject=entity:<6> predicate=responsible_for object="routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, note/claim extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, and retention" family=responsible_for policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 10.2 status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The Memory worker / manager is responsible for routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, note/claim extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, and retention.
- id=hot-path:claim:<1> subject=entity:<7> predicate=improves_memory_reconciliation_and_retrieval object="Retrieval/reconciliation prevents the memory database from accumulating duplicates, stale preferences, conflicting notes, task explosion, and summary spam." family=improves_memory_reconciliation_and_retrieval policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 9 status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Memory retrieval and reconciliation prevents database growth in duplicates, stale preferences, conflicting notes, task explosion, and summary spam.
- id=hot-path:claim:<2> subject=entity:<8> predicate=repairs_memory_degradation object="duplicate clusters, stale items, contradictory active items, note spam, profile drift, and bad merges" family=repairs_memory_degradation policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 3 status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Repair fixes memory degradation such as duplicate clusters, stale items, contradictory active items, note spam, profile drift, and bad merges.
- id=hot-path:claim:<3> subject=entity:<9> predicate=indexes_memory_content_for_retrieval object="vector indexes, full-text indexes, and filters for sources, notes, claims, episodes, content text, summaries, normalized text, namespace, entity, status, retrieval state, time, and type" family=indexes_memory_content_for_retrieval policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to the storage-tier architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The Retrieval indexes storage tier indexes memory content for retrieval using vector indexes, full-text indexes, and filters.
- id=hot-path:claim:<4> subject=entity:<10> predicate=converts_mature_notes_to_structured_memory object="claims, tasks, profile, or episodes" family=converts_mature_notes_to_structured_memory policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 3 status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Consolidation converts mature notes into claims, tasks, profile, or episodes when the signal is strong enough.
- id=hot-path:claim:<5> subject=entity:<11> predicate=has_goal object="store facts, preferences, tasks, rationale, and evolving plans" family=has_goal policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 1 goal of the system status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The AI agent memory layer is intended to store facts, preferences, tasks, rationale, and evolving plans.
- id=hot-path:claim:<6> subject=entity:<12> predicate=should_be_written_when object="Information should be written as a Note when it is already important, useful for future retrieval, but too context-rich or too early for a hard fact." family=should_be_written_when policy=cardinality=SINGLE,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=REPLACE scope=Applies to the memory architecture rules in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=A Note should be written when information is already important and useful for future retrieval, but too context-rich or too early for a hard fact.
- id=hot-path:claim:<7> subject=entity:<13> predicate=primary_storage_backend object=entity:<14> family=primary_storage_backend policy=cardinality=SINGLE,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=REPLACE scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 4 recommended architecture status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The recommended memory architecture uses Postgres + pgvector as the primary storage backend.
- id=hot-path:claim:<8> subject=entity:<15> predicate=excludes_from_tier object="all claims, all notes, raw transcript, and long fact lists" family=excludes_from_tier policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to the storage-tier architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The Core / in-context memory storage tier excludes all claims, all notes, raw transcript, and long fact lists.
- id=hot-path:claim:<9> subject=entity:<13> predicate=has_constraint object="When one material contains explicit facts or tasks plus rationale or direction, both Claim and Note should be written." family=has_constraint policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to the memory architecture rules in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=When one material contains explicit facts or tasks plus rationale or direction, both Claim and Note should be written.
- id=hot-path:claim:<10> subject=entity:<16> predicate=has_constraint object="without its four axes, memory quickly becomes either a transcript dump or a brittle fact store" family=has_constraint policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 2 central architecture idea status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Memory v3 architecture treats the four-axis design as necessary to avoid memory becoming either a transcript dump or a brittle fact store.
- id=hot-path:claim:<11> subject=entity:<17> predicate=improves_memory_reconciliation_and_retrieval object="predictable reconciliation, less overgeneralized retrieval, and fewer false note conflicts" family=improves_memory_reconciliation_and_retrieval policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to structured scope in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Structured scope improves memory behavior by making reconciliation more predictable, reducing retrieval overgeneralization, and preventing false note conflicts.
- id=hot-path:claim:<12> subject=entity:<18> predicate=serves_as_intermediate_semantic_layer object="rich note memory for design direction, rationale, local conclusions, plan fragments, document digests, and lessons" family=serves_as_intermediate_semantic_layer policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to the storage-tier architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The Rich note memory storage tier serves as an intermediate semantic layer for design direction, rationale, local conclusions, plan fragments, document digests, and lessons.
- id=hot-path:claim:<13> subject=entity:<16> predicate=has_semantic_write_path object=entity:<12> family=has_semantic_write_path policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 3 status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The memory system has Note path as a semantic write path.

        Predicate catalog excerpt:
        - answers_question: Question or semantic role answered by a state concept.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=REPLACE; importance=5
- assigned_to_shift: Person or agent assignment to a shift, schedule slot, day, route, or rotation cell; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- attended_event: Subject attended or was present at a dated event, appointment, meeting, or gathering; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=COEXIST; importance=6
- avoids: Negative preference, exclusion, or avoid instruction; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- believes: Attributed belief, opinion, or viewpoint held by the subject; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- can_share_physical_storage_backend: Component or tier may physically reside in the same backend as other storage.; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- converts_mature_notes_to_structured_memory: A maintenance path converts mature notes into structured memory when criteria are met.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- current_location: Current physical residence, base, or location of a person, user, organization, project, or concrete subject; subject=any; object=JSON; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=8
- current_metric_value: Current value of a named metric, record, benchmark, score, quota, threshold, or personal best; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=8
- excludes_from_tier: Storage tier explicitly excludes specified content types.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- generated_artifact_detail: Concrete reusable detail contained in an imported generated artifact, draft, story, image description, specification, or code artifact; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=6
- has_architecture_axis: Learned predicate for Memory v3 architecture has Representation as one of its four axes.; subject=any; object=JSON; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- has_constraint: Stable user or project constraint; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=8
- has_experience_with: Experience with a technology or domain; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=6
- has_goal: Active or declared goal; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=8
- has_maintenance_path: Architecture includes a maintenance path option.; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- has_semantic_write_path: Architecture includes a semantic write path option.; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- improves_memory_reconciliation_and_retrieval: Design feature improves reconciliation, retrieval precision, or conflict behavior.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- indexes_memory_content_for_retrieval: Storage tier provides retrieval indexes and filters over memory content.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- owns_vehicle: Vehicle owned or used by the subject; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=6
- pending_pickup: Subject needs to collect, pick up, retrieve, or receive a concrete item from a place or party; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=8
- pending_return: Subject needs to return, send back, drop off, or bring back a concrete item to a place or party; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=8
- preferred_name: Preferred display name for the subject; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=REPLACE; importance=8
- prefers: Generic positive preference when no named current/default/primary/chosen slot predicate is more precise; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- prefers_answer_detail: Preferred answer detail level; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=8
- prefers_code_comment_language: Preferred language for code comments; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=9
- prefers_framework: Preferred framework or library; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=6
- prefers_response_language: Preferred language for assistant responses; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=9
- prefers_storage: Preferred storage solution; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- prefers_tone: Preferred response tone; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=7
- primary_storage_backend: Primary storage or persistence backend for a project or service; subject=PROJECT; object=ENTITY; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=8
- repairs_memory_degradation: A maintenance path repairs specified classes of memory degradation.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- requires_scope_representations: Structured scope requires specified human-readable and machine-readable representations.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- responsible_for: Subject owns or is responsible for a workstream, component, process, account duty, or operational area; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=8
- serves_as_intermediate_semantic_layer: Storage tier functions as an intermediate semantic layer for specified note-like uses.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- serves_as_typed_memory_store: Storage tier functions as the main typed memory store.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=REPLACE; importance=5
- should_be_written_when: Criteria under which a memory item type should be written.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=5
- speaks_language: Language known or used by the subject; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- stores_raw_evidence: Storage tier stores raw evidence records, usually immutably.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- supplies_prompt_context: Storage tier provides content intended to be included in prompt context.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- timezone: Timezone of the subject; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=7
- uses_persistence_database: Persistence database used by a project or service in a specific environment, phase, or time window; subject=PROJECT; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=RANGE_SPLIT; importance=6
- uses_search_backend: Search backend used by a project or service in a specific environment, phase, or time window; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=RANGE_SPLIT; importance=7
- works_on_project: Association with a project when no more specific role, responsibility, ownership, or project-state predicate is available; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=RANGE_SPLIT; importance=7
- works_with: Current or ongoing technology/tool usage; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7

responseFormat.description: Reconcile claim candidates against active memory claims.
responseFormat.name: memory_claim_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

content.text:
Memory stage: ClaimReconciler v1.
        Runtime processing time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate claims should update active memory and assign predicate policy.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_claim_id": "existing-claim-id-or-null",
              "canonical_predicate": "stable_snake_case_predicate_or_null",
              "predicate_family": "stable_conceptual_family_or_null",
              "predicate_description": "short policy meaning or null",
              "object_kind": "entity | string | number | boolean | json | null",
              "cardinality": "single | multi | null",
              "temporal_policy": "atemporal | time_scoped | status_like | null",
              "conflict_policy": "replace | coexist | range_split | null",
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new durable information.
        - noop: candidate is duplicate, low-value, or should not become active memory.
        - supersede: candidate is the new current value and target_claim_id is the old active claim it replaces.
        - retract: target_claim_id should stop being active because the target message retracts it without a replacement candidate.
        - update: target_claim_id should be patched without adding a new candidate; use sparingly.

        Predicate policy fields:
        - canonical_predicate: normalized predicate to store for the candidate. Reuse an existing predicate only if it is the same semantic relation; otherwise choose a concise snake_case relation.
        - predicate_family: stable conceptual family used for future reconciliation.
        - cardinality: single if one subject can have only one active value in this family and scope; multi if several values can coexist.
        - temporal_policy: atemporal for timeless facts, time_scoped for facts true in explicit time windows, status_like for current states, current preferences, decisions, and other evolving values.
        - conflict_policy: replace when a new value invalidates the old active value, coexist when both can remain true, range_split when old and new values are both temporally meaningful.

        Rules:
        - Use the target source and full thread context to understand corrections, replacements, pronouns, temporal meaning, and scope.
        - Do not decide by literal predicate names. Infer the semantic predicate family from the claim meaning, subject, object, qualifiers, evidence, scope, and temporal fields.
        - Use an existing predicate from Predicate catalog excerpt when it captures the same semantic relation.
        - If a catalog predicate fits, set canonical_predicate to that exact predicate and copy its cardinality, temporal_policy, and conflict_policy.
        - Invent a new canonical_predicate only when no active catalog predicate captures the relation.
        - Never canonicalize to generic description, classification, property-bag, or paraphrase predicates such as "is_described_as", "has_property", "is_a", or "defined_as". Choose a specific durable relation or return noop.
        - For ordinary positive preferences, use catalog predicate "prefers"; do not keep category-specific variants such as "prefers_car_brand" unless they represent a named current/default slot.
        - Do not map a named current/default/primary/chosen/backend slot to a generic preference, affinity, status, or usage predicate; use or create the specific slot predicate.
        - For named metric, record, score, benchmark, quota, threshold, or personal-best values, use catalog predicate "current_metric_value" when present. Preserve the metric/domain in scope or qualifiers so unrelated metrics do not replace each other.
        - For current place, residence, base, or relocation-result values, use catalog predicate "current_location" when present. It is a status-like single-value slot for the same subject and scope; newer explicit current-location values replace older active values.
        - For concrete details inside imported generated artifacts, use catalog predicate "generated_artifact_detail" when no more specific catalog predicate fits. These details usually coexist unless a later artifact version explicitly supersedes an earlier version.
        - If the target source updates or replaces an existing value, keep the semantic slot/family from the replaced claim when the source and context identify that slot.
        - If multiple candidates from the same target source express the same durable point at different abstraction levels, keep the most specific/actionable candidate and return noop for weaker broader candidates.
        - If one candidate captures the target source's explicit current/default/status value and another candidate is only a generic preference or affinity inferred from the same words, return noop for the generic candidate unless the target source independently asserts it.
        - Do not insert redundant broader candidates merely because they use a different predicate or family.
        - For exclusive current/default/status values in the same subject, scope, and environment, use cardinality=single and conflict_policy=replace unless simultaneous active values are explicitly allowed.
        - For dated observations or historical phase facts, use temporal_policy=time_scoped; they can coexist with later current-state claims when scopes differ by time, phase, or environment.
        - Runtime processing time is not evidence that a pending obligation has expired, completed, or become stale. For catalog pending_* claims and other independently completeable duties, insert the explicit pending fact unless the target source or existing active memory explicitly says it was completed, cancelled, returned, picked up, superseded, or otherwise no longer pending.
        - If the candidate is equivalent to an existing active claim in the same semantic family and scope, return noop.
        - If predicate policy says replace and the candidate conflicts with an older active value in the same family and scope, supersede the old claim.
        - If predicate policy says coexist, insert the candidate unless it is a duplicate.
        - If predicate policy says range_split, insert the candidate and supersede only an old current-state claim that should no longer be retrieved as current.
        - Attributed viewpoints, independent owners, independent events, and historical facts usually coexist unless the target source explicitly retracts them.
        - Use catalog predicate "believes" for third-party viewpoints such as "Alice thinks X"; do not canonicalize those claims to "prefers" or "avoids" unless the source explicitly asserts preference or avoidance.
        - Prefer insert over destructive actions when policy or evidence is unclear.
        - Return one operation for each candidate unless the right action is a target-only retract/update.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate claims below.
        - target_claim_id must be one of Existing active claims below or null.
        - Fill predicate policy fields for every operation with a candidate, including noop.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 11. Write path .. 12. Read path
Document lines: 329-466

## 11. Write path

### Step 1. Persist source
Сохраняем raw input:
- text
- source type
- speaker
- timestamp
- namespace
- thread/conversation ids
- optional embedding

### Step 2. Route
Router решает:
- `noop`
- `direct_structured_write`
- `note_write`
- `mixed`

### Step 3. Retrieve candidate memories
Перед extraction достаем:
- похожие claims
- похожие notes
- open tasks
- existing profile
- candidate entities / aliases
- recent evidence

### Step 4. Canonicalize entities
Привязать mentions к existing entities или создать новые.

### Step 5. Extract
Разные prompts для:
- claim extraction
- note construction
- task update
- profile update

### Step 6. Reconcile
Сравниваем candidate items с existing items.

### Step 7. Apply transaction
Одной транзакцией:
- upsert entities
- apply claim ops
- apply note ops
- apply task ops
- update profile projection
- store memory run log

### Step 8. Schedule maintenance
По debounce или cron:
- note consolidation
- duplicate merge
- stale cleanup
- profile repair
- alias merge
- retention / archive

## 12. Read path

### Step 1. Decide whether memory is needed
Memory search нужен, если вопрос:
- user-specific
- history-aware
- task-aware
- rationale-heavy
- temporal
- high-stakes

### Step 2. Load core blocks
Подтягиваем:
- short profile
- active tasks
- optional session summary

### Step 3. Choose retrieval mode

#### Claim-first
Для:
- factual questions
- preferences
- current status
- short “кто/что/когда”

#### Note-first
Для:
- “почему решили?”
- “к чему склонялись?”
- “что обсуждали?”
- “какой паттерн уже был?”

#### Mixed
Для:
- factual + rationale
- history + current state
- task with supporting discussion

#### Evidence-first / evidence-fallback
Для:
- конфликтов
- запросов на подтверждение / цитату
- высокой ставки

### Step 4. Respect retrieval budget
`v3` требует явных budget caps.

Рекомендуемый старт:

- `factual`
  - up to `6 claims`
  - up to `2 tasks`
  - up to `2 evidence snippets`

- `rationale`
  - up to `4 notes`
  - up to `2 claims`
  - up to `4 evidence snippets`

- `task`
  - up to `4 tasks`
  - up to `2 notes`
  - up to `2 evidence snippets`

- `mixed`
  - up to `4 claims`
  - up to `3 notes`
  - up to `2 tasks`
  - up to `3 evidence snippets`

### Step 5. Compose answer
Answer composer обязан:
- различать verified vs provisional
- учитывать conflicts
- уважать scope boundaries
- уметь abstain
- не выдавать note как hard fact без оговорки

- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<2> predicate=answers_question object="factual questions, preferences, current status, and short who/what/when questions" scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 12 Read path validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Claim-first retrieval mode is used for factual questions, preferences, current status, and short who/what/when questions. evidence=Claim-first Для: - factual questions - preferences - current status - short “кто/что/когда”
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<3> predicate=answers_question object="why a decision was made, what the system leaned toward, what was discussed, and what pattern already existed" scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 12 Read path validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Note-first retrieval mode is used for rationale, prior leanings, discussion history, and previously observed patterns. evidence=Note-first Для: - “почему решили?” - “к чему склонялись?” - “что обсуждали?” - “какой паттерн уже был?”
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<4> predicate=answers_question object="questions combining facts with rationale, history with current state, or tasks with supporting discussion" scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 12 Read path validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Mixed retrieval mode is used for questions combining factual and rationale needs, history and current state, or tasks with supporting discussion. evidence=Mixed Для: - factual + rationale - history + current state - task with supporting discussion
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<5> predicate=answers_question object="conflicts, requests for confirmation or quotation, and high-stakes questions" scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 12 Read path validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Evidence-first or evidence-fallback retrieval mode is used for conflicts, confirmation or quote requests, and high-stakes questions. evidence=Evidence-first / evidence-fallback Для: - конфликтов - запросов на подтверждение / цитату - высокой ставки

- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<1> predicate=has_constraint object="The Read path answer composer must distinguish verified versus provisional information, account for conflicts, respect scope boundaries, be able to abstain, and avoid presenting a note as a hard fact without qualification." scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 12 Read path validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The Read path answer composer must distinguish verified from provisional information, account for conflicts, respect scope boundaries, abstain when needed, and avoid treating notes as hard facts without qualification. evidence=Answer composer обязан: - различать verified vs provisional - учитывать conflicts - уважать scope boundaries - уметь abstain - не выдавать note как hard fact без оговорки

        Existing active claims:

- id=hot-path:claim:<4> subject=entity:<10> predicate=converts_mature_notes_to_structured_memory object="claims, tasks, profile, or episodes" family=converts_mature_notes_to_structured_memory policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 3 status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Consolidation converts mature notes into claims, tasks, profile, or episodes when the signal is strong enough.

- id=hot-path:claim:<8> subject=entity:<15> predicate=excludes_from_tier object="all claims, all notes, raw transcript, and long fact lists" family=excludes_from_tier policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to the storage-tier architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The Core / in-context memory storage tier excludes all claims, all notes, raw transcript, and long fact lists.
- id=hot-path:claim:<9> subject=entity:<13> predicate=has_constraint object="When one material contains explicit facts or tasks plus rationale or direction, both Claim and Note should be written." family=has_constraint policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST scope=Applies to the memory architecture rules in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md. status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=When one material contains explicit facts or tasks plus rationale or direction, both Claim and Note should be written.
- id=hot-path:claim:<10> subject=entity:<16> predicate=has_constraint object="without its four axes, memory quickly becomes either a transcript dump or a brittle fact store" family=has_constraint policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 2 central architecture idea status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Memory v3 architecture treats the four-axis design as necessary to avoid memory becoming either a transcript dump or a brittle fact store.

        Predicate catalog excerpt:
        - answers_question: Question or semantic role answered by a state concept.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=REPLACE; importance=5
- assigned_to_shift: Person or agent assignment to a shift, schedule slot, day, route, or rotation cell; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- attended_event: Subject attended or was present at a dated event, appointment, meeting, or gathering; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=COEXIST; importance=6
- avoids: Negative preference, exclusion, or avoid instruction; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- believes: Attributed belief, opinion, or viewpoint held by the subject; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- can_share_physical_storage_backend: Component or tier may physically reside in the same backend as other storage.; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- converts_mature_notes_to_structured_memory: A maintenance path converts mature notes into structured memory when criteria are met.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- current_location: Current physical residence, base, or location of a person, user, organization, project, or concrete subject; subject=any; object=JSON; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=8
- current_metric_value: Current value of a named metric, record, benchmark, score, quota, threshold, or personal best; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=8
- excludes_from_tier: Storage tier explicitly excludes specified content types.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- generated_artifact_detail: Concrete reusable detail contained in an imported generated artifact, draft, story, image description, specification, or code artifact; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=6
- has_architecture_axis: Learned predicate for Memory v3 architecture has Representation as one of its four axes.; subject=any; object=JSON; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- has_constraint: Stable user or project constraint; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=8
- has_experience_with: Experience with a technology or domain; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=6
- has_goal: Active or declared goal; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=8
- has_maintenance_path: Architecture includes a maintenance path option.; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- has_semantic_write_path: Architecture includes a semantic write path option.; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- improves_memory_reconciliation_and_retrieval: Design feature improves reconciliation, retrieval precision, or conflict behavior.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- indexes_memory_content_for_retrieval: Storage tier provides retrieval indexes and filters over memory content.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- owns_vehicle: Vehicle owned or used by the subject; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=6
- pending_pickup: Subject needs to collect, pick up, retrieve, or receive a concrete item from a place or party; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=8
- pending_return: Subject needs to return, send back, drop off, or bring back a concrete item to a place or party; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=8
- preferred_name: Preferred display name for the subject; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=REPLACE; importance=8
- prefers: Generic positive preference when no named current/default/primary/chosen slot predicate is more precise; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- prefers_answer_detail: Preferred answer detail level; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=8
- prefers_code_comment_language: Preferred language for code comments; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=9
- prefers_framework: Preferred framework or library; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=6
- prefers_response_language: Preferred language for assistant responses; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=9
- prefers_storage: Preferred storage solution; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- prefers_tone: Preferred response tone; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=7
- primary_storage_backend: Primary storage or persistence backend for a project or service; subject=PROJECT; object=ENTITY; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=8
- repairs_memory_degradation: A maintenance path repairs specified classes of memory degradation.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- requires_scope_representations: Structured scope requires specified human-readable and machine-readable representations.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- responsible_for: Subject owns or is responsible for a workstream, component, process, account duty, or operational area; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=8
- serves_as_intermediate_semantic_layer: Storage tier functions as an intermediate semantic layer for specified note-like uses.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- serves_as_typed_memory_store: Storage tier functions as the main typed memory store.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=REPLACE; importance=5
- should_be_written_when: Criteria under which a memory item type should be written.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=5
- speaks_language: Language known or used by the subject; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7
- stores_raw_evidence: Storage tier stores raw evidence records, usually immutably.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- supplies_prompt_context: Storage tier provides content intended to be included in prompt context.; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; importance=5
- timezone: Timezone of the subject; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; importance=7
- uses_persistence_database: Persistence database used by a project or service in a specific environment, phase, or time window; subject=PROJECT; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=RANGE_SPLIT; importance=6
- uses_search_backend: Search backend used by a project or service in a specific environment, phase, or time window; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=RANGE_SPLIT; importance=7
- works_on_project: Association with a project when no more specific role, responsibility, ownership, or project-state predicate is available; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=RANGE_SPLIT; importance=7
- works_with: Current or ongoing technology/tool usage; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; importance=7

responseFormat.name: memory_claim_reconciler
responseFormat.description: Reconcile claim candidates against active memory claims.
